perturbation.original_text: This promotion shall begin on June 1,1999 and shall terminate June 1, 2000 (herein "Term") This term shall be extended for a 1 year period provided 3000 pagers per month are distributed to Purchase customers.
perturbation.changed_text: This promotion shall begin on June 1,1999 and may terminate June 1, 2000 (herein "Term"). This term may be extended for a 1 year period if deemed beneficial by PageMaster.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "shall terminate" to "may terminate" and replacing the objective extension condition (3000 pagers/month) with a subjective assessment by PageMaster, this creates ambiguity. Section 3 now contradicts other sections that assume a fixed term for the promotion and related responsibilities. This makes the duration of the term uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Responsibilities of PageMaster Corporation ------------------------------------------ PageMaster Corporation shall be responsible for providing the following: a. For Purchase Customers to participate in the promotion, PageMaster Corporation shall establish and maintain a toll-free telephone number for this promotion beginning June 1,1999 and continuing until September 1, 2000 unless otherwise requested by Go Call and agreed upon by PageMaster Corporation. b. PageMaster Corporation shall provide a minimum of 100,000 up to 500,000 pagers for the fulfillment of this promotion to all Purchase Customers who prepay their annual airtime. c. PageMaster Corporation shall be responsible for all fulfillment obligations of this promotion relating to paging services, including, but not limited to, timely delivery of pagers, paging services, defective goods handling, subcontracting, deadlines, and handling of consumer and regulatory inquiries and complaints. d. PageMaster Corporation will contract with a nationwide airtime service provider to fulfill and to ship Purchasing Customer orders direct to the Purchase Customers to fulfill this promotion in a timely manner. PageMaster Corporation has chosen for the purpose of this promotion, MetroCall Inc. to provide pager and airtime services where the nationwide airtime service provider has the facilities and the requisite governmental authority to provide such services. All Purchase Customers shall become customers of the nationwide airtime service provider. The nationwide airtime service provider shall be allowed to market additional pagers arid enhanced services to all Purchase Customers, and to charge for over-calls with respect to any account with a Purchase Customer. The nationwide airtime service provider shall be able to discontinue or terminate service to any Purchase Customer in accordance with the terms of the contract between the nationwide airtime service provider and the Purchase Customer. Notwithstanding the foregoing, PageMaster Corporation shall remain solely responsible for the fulfillment of all services and obligations set forth in this Agreement. e. PageMaster Corporation shall not engage in the same or similar promotion with any other On-Line Casinos from June 1, 1999 through June 1, 2000. f. PageMaster Corporation will provide at no charge programming software that will allow Go Call to broadcast any and all messages of 125 characters or less to all Go Call consumers who have redeemed pagers on this promotion.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a fixed termination date ('shall terminate June 1, 2000') and objective extension conditions. The changed text replaces this with a flexible termination ('may terminate June 1, 2000') and subjective extension criteria ('if deemed beneficial by PageMaster'). This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines PageMaster's responsibilities based on a term extending to at least September 1, 2000. Because the term's duration is now uncertain based on PageMaster's assessment, the contradicted text which assumes fixed responsibilities for a defined period becomes unenforceable, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: PageMaster Corporation shall pay to Go Call, Co-Op Marketing funds for the promotion. PageMaster Corporation will pay Go Call $3.00 per pager (beginning with pager # 1) and 5% of all airtime renewal revenue for each pager redeemed for this promotion consistent with the terms of paragraph 6b of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: PageMaster Corporation will make a good faith effort to pay Co-Op Marketing funds for the promotion. PageMaster Corporation will pay Go Call up to $3.00 per pager and a negotiable percentage of airtime renewal revenue for each pager redeemed for this promotion, subject to PageMaster's budgetary constraints and market conditions.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "shall pay" to "will make a good faith effort to pay," and modifying the fixed amounts to "up to $3.00 per pager and a negotiable percentage", introduce an in-text contradiction related to co-op marketing funds. This weakens the obligation and introduces uncertainty. This creates ambiguity because the original text implies a guaranteed payment structure, while the modified text allows PageMaster discretion based on undefined criteria, thus contraticting the fixed payment scheme previously outlined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: On the last day of each month, the refund of Go Call's portion of the deposit shall be calculated by PageMaster Corporation for the &bbsp; prior month and will be forwarded to Go Call by check, along with an extended accounting of all pagers and customers until September 1, 2000, unless otherwise instructed by Go Call. Go Call, upon ten (10) days written notice, shall have the right to examine the books and records of PageMaster Corporation to verify the sales resulting from this promotion. Such examination shall be made at the regular place of business of PageMaster Corporation where such books and records are maintained during normal business hours and shall be conducted at Go Call's expense by a certified public accountant or other Go Call executive so designated by Go Call.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity and weakens the obligation for PageMaster to pay Go Call marketing funds. The original text specifies a fixed payment per pager and a percentage of airtime renewal revenue. The modified text changes 'shall pay' to 'will make a good faith effort to pay' and alters the fixed amounts to 'up to $3.00 per pager and a negotiable percentage,' making the payment dependent on PageMaster's 'budgetary constraints and market conditions.' This directly contradicts the expectation of a calculated and guaranteed payment outlined in the 'Contradicted Text,' which describes the monthly calculation and forwarding of Go Call's portion of the deposit based on pager sales. The original text assumes a calculable amount due to the fixed payment structure, while the changed text makes the payment variable and subject to PageMaster's discretion, conflicting with the accounting process described in the contradicted text.
